page: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 16 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per vCPU. The server is equipped with two NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace family, providing a combined total of 48 GB of VRAM.

The hardware configuration presents specific architectural tradeoffs. While the system offers a substantial memory capacity of 192.0 GB and dual hardware accelerators, the 16 vCPUs are allocated on a shared basis. Additionally, this server type does not include bundled local storage, meaning storage resources must be attached externally. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included.

This server is designed for workloads that require significant GPU acceleration and large memory footprints but can tolerate shared CPU resources. Suitable applications include machine learning inference, data science tasks, and GPU-accelerated rendering or analytics where local storage is not a primary requirement.
description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 16 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high allocation of 12.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs, delivering a total of 48 GB of VRAM. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and the absence of integrated local storage, requiring external storage solutions. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive GPU workloads, such as machine learning inference, data science, and parallel processing tasks, where dedicated CPU performance is not the primary bottleneck.
